doc_description: A USDA Rural Development guide to the Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program, explaining eligibility, property requirements, and no-down-payment financing options for approved rural home loans.

structure.title: Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program
structure.summary: The document explains USDA Rural Development’s Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G), which helps approved lenders provide 100% financed, no-money-down loans to eligible low- and moderate-income borrowers. It outlines what the program is used for, including buying an existing home, building a new home, or financing rehabilitation/improvements/relocation of a home. It also describes who can apply, such as borrowers who will occupy the home as a primary residence, meet income limits, cannot obtain conventional financing without PMI, and satisfy citizenship/legal requirements. The document summarizes eligible property requirements, noting that homes must be in eligible rural areas and that loan amounts are based on repayment ability and appraised value rather than purchase price. It highlights key loan terms, such as no down payment, no limit on gift funds, fixed 30-year interest rates, and the ability to include certain closing and related costs in financing. Finally, it explains that borrowers must work with approved USDA lenders and provides references to the governing regulations and handbook, along with a note to consult official sources for updated information.

pages.content:
Together , America Prospers 
Single Family Housing Guaranteed Loan Program 
What does this 
program do? 
The Single Family Housing 
Guaranteed Loan Program (SFH-G) helps approved lenders offer 100 percent-fnanced, no-money-down loans to eligible low-and moderate-income people. 
Backed by USDA Rural 
Development, the 90-percent loan guarantee encourages lender participation by minimizing risk. 
With a Single Family Housing 
loan guarantee, you can buy an existing home, cover the cost of rehabilitating, improving, or relocating a home, or build new. 
This program is not restricted to 
frst-time homebuyers. 
QR code to the SFH-G webpage Who can apply? 
Among other requirements, you must: • Agree to occupy the home as your 
primary residence 
• Meet median household income requirements for the area in which the eligible home is located. You will fnd income eligibility information at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-income. 
• Be unable to get conventional fnancing with no private mortgage insurance (PMI) 
• Meet U.S. citizen or noncitizen requirements, and not be suspended or debarred from participating in federal programs 
What types of properties are eligible? 
Among other requirements, single-
family dwellings must be located in eligible rural areas. A property eligibility map is available at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-property. 
• Loans are based on repayment 
ability , and there is no set maximum purchase price or acreage limit as long as acreage is considered common for the area. 
• Provided they are otherwise eligible, “fipped” properties are allowed. What are some other loan terms? 
• You must have stable, dependable 
income and demonstrate a willingness and ability to repay debt. 
• No down payment is required, and there is no limit on gift funds. 
• Fixed, 30-year-interest rates are negotiated between you and your approved lender . 
• Loans are based on a property’s appraised value, not purchase price. Thus, you potentially can also include closing costs, home repair expenses, and such items as utilities connection fees, tax and insurance escrow, essential household equipment, and site preparation in your fnancing. 
How do we get started? 
• You must work directly with 
approved USDA RD lenders. A state-based list is available at this link: rd.usda.gov/resources/lenders 
What governs this program? 
• 7-CFR Part 3555, available at this 
link: tinyurl.com/7CFR-3555 
• The SFH-G Handbook is available at this link: tinyurl.com/SFHG-HB - PDF 
NOTE: Because information changes, always consult offcial program instructions 
or contact your local Rural Development offce for help. A list is available at this link: go.usa.gov/xJHPE. You will fnd additional resources, forms, and program information at rd.usda.gov. USDA is an equal opportunity provider, employer, and lender. 
Last revised October 2024
